request.question: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

clarifying_questions: What is the numeric success threshold for improvement in viability?

references.title: Trehalose preincubation increases mesenchymal (CD271 + ) stem cells post-cryopreservation viability
references.relevance_note: Examines the effects of trehalose preincubation on post-cryopreservation viability, relevant to the proposed intervention of adding trehalose.

literature_qc.explanation: In the intervention dimension, while the specific concentration of trehalose (250mM) isn't directly covered, paper [4] discusses trehalose preincubation in the context of improving post-cryopreservation viability, which is closely related. The biological system dimension is addressed by utilizing HeLa cells, as paper [4] relates to stem cells, which, while different, share similarities with the cell lines being studied. Lastly, the experiment type of comparative cryopreservation is relevant as the methodologies discussed in other studies involve comparative aspects of cryopreservation techniques, specifically with DMSO.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To compare post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with 250 mM trehalose in freezing medium versus DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different cryoprotectants: 250 mM trehalose and DMSO. Each cryoprotectant group will include three replicates to assess post-thaw viability using trypan blue exclusion method after a recovery period. The experiment will include a control group treated with no cryoprotectant.
controls: HeLa cells subjected to DMSO freezing medium at a concentration of 10% (v/v) as per standard cryopreservation protocol.
HeLa cells not subjected to cryopreservation for viability baseline measurement.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Thaw a vial of HeLa cells in a 37°C water bath for 2-3 min until only a small ice crystal remains.
Expected outcome: Cells are completely thawed and ready for culturing.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Transfer the thawed cells into a 15 mL tube containing 10 mL of growth medium and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet the cells.
Expected outcome: Pelleted cells at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Resuspend the pelleted cells in 10 mL of growth medium and count using a hemocytometer to achieve a concentration of 1 × 10^6 cells/mL.
Expected outcome: Cell concentration is accurate at 1 × 10^6 cells/mL.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Cryoprotectant Preparation
Prepare freezing medium by adding 250 mM trehalose to DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin/streptomycin.
Expected outcome: Homogeneous freezing medium containing 250 mM trehalose is ready.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Procedure
Aliquot 1 mL of HeLa cell suspension into two separate 1.5 mL cryovials for the trehalose and DMSO groups.
Expected outcome: Two cryovials each contain 1 mL of cell suspension.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Procedure
Add 1 mL of the trehalose freezing medium to one cryovial and add 1 mL of 10% DMSO to the other.
Expected outcome: Each cryovial contains 1 mL of the corresponding cryoprotectant.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Cryopreservation Procedure
Place each cryovial in Mr. Frosty freezing container and store at -80°C for 24 h.
Expected outcome: Cryovials are adequately cooled at -80°C.

DAY 1 | 5 min | Thawing Process
Retrieve cryovials from Mr. Frosty and immediately immerse in a 37°C water bath for 2-3 min to thaw.
Expected outcome: Cryovials are thawed without ice crystals.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Cell Recovery
Transfer thawed cell suspension to a 15 mL tube, add 10 mL of growth medium, and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet cells.
Expected outcome: Cells are pelleted and ready for resuspension.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Recovery
Resuspend cells in 2 mL of growth medium and incubate at 37°C for 30 min to recover cells post-thaw.
Expected outcome: Cells have recovered and are ready for viability assessment.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Viability Assessment
Perform trypan blue exclusion staining by combining 100 µL of sample with 100 µL of trypan blue solution and counting under a microscope.
Expected outcome: Viability is determined based on the number of blue (dead) versus clear (alive) cells.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Data Analysis
Calculate post-thaw viability percentage of cells for trehalose and DMSO groups and compare using Student's t-test.
Expected outcome: Statistical comparison of post-thaw viability yields p-value for significance.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: HeLa cells maintain viability under the conditions tested for cryopreservation.
Trehalose effectively protects HeLa cells from cryoinjury as previously reported in related studies.
Cell counts using trypan blue will accurately reflect live versus dead cells.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability for trehalose is significantly greater than DMSO with p < 0.05.
Determine if the average viability percentage for trehalose is ≥ 75% post-thaw recovery.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Standardize growth batch of HeLa cells within 3 passages across experiments.
Ensure specific lot numbers for trehalose and DMSO to avoid variability in cryoprotection.
Use consistent media formulations across all experiments.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. Add Mr. Frosty step
3. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To compare post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with 250 mM trehalose in freezing medium versus DMSO.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
